article.description: David West, the two-time conference player of the year, scored 21 points as Xavier (No. 23 ESPN/USA Today, No. 24 AP) beat Dayton 66-59 Friday night to earn a spot in the championship game.

related.headline: Spiders reach final in first year in A-10

article.headline: After slow tourney start, West heats up
article.linkText: West delivers as Xavier lands in Atlantic-10 final
article.story:
PHILADELPHIA (AP) -- It took more than 60 minutes of basketball
in the Atlantic 10 tournament for David West to finally start
playing as expected.

	   West, the two-time conference player of the year, scored 21
points as Xavier (No. 23 ESPN/USA Today, No. 24 AP) beat Dayton 66-59 Friday night to earn a spot in the championship game.

	   "In the second half, with our shot selection and our patience,
we really got David involved," Xavier coach Thad Matta said. "We
played off David."

	   Romain Sato added 16 points for Xavier (24-5), which took
control with a 7-0 spurt in the second half.

	   Brooks Hall hit a 3-pointer with 1:07 left to get Dayton within
four, but Sato answered with a 3 from the wing with 39 seconds to
go. West sealed it at the foul line with a pair of free throws.

	   West shot 2-for-10 from the field in the first half but 5-for-7
in the second half. He was 7-for-8 from the free-throw line and
added nine rebounds.

	   "I picked up my third foul early in the second half, but if I
look not to foul, it doesn't help things," West said. "So I just
went out and played my game. I didn't worry about fouling at all. I
played aggressive."

	   Dayton coach Oliver Purnell credited Xavier's guards with
getting West the ball in the right situations.

	   "The passers made great passes and gave him easy baskets,"
Purnell said. "We made some defensive errors, had problems
switching, and that led to easy shots."

   Keith Waleskowski led Dayton (20-10) with 16 points. Hall added
14, and Sean Finn had 13.

	   The second half got off to a fast start, and Dayton used a 14-5
run to take a 40-37 lead.

	   "They scored 16 points in four minutes, and if they continued
they would have set an NCAA scoring record," Matta said. "They
were getting a lot of layups and we had to get back on transition.
We finally slowed them down after that. I think it was us playing
not to lose, not to win."

	   The game was tied at 44 after Finn's two-handed, fast-break slam
-- a dunk so powerful the shot clock above the backboard went dark.
The game resumed after an 18-minute delay with replacement shot
clocks.

	   After Dayton got a bucket from Ramod Marshall, Xavier responded
with seven straight points -- part of a 16-6 run that gave the
Musketeers a 60-52 lead with 1:56 to go.

	   West scored nine points during the surge.

	   "We haven't been hitting our shots and these rims aren't
helping us much," West said. "They have been collapsing on me a
lot."

	   Dayton shot 38 percent for the game and went 5-for-16 from
behind the 3-point line. Xavier shot 49 percent from the field -- 74
percent in the second half.

	   "We came out last night and missed a lot, and the same thing
happened tonight," Sato said. "You do what coach says -- keep
shooting. The shots can come. You don't force it."

	   Dave Young, who has started every game this season for Xavier,
didn't play because of a hairline fracture in his right hand. Alvin
Brown started in his place and scored five points in 14 minutes.

	   Dayton built a 13-5 lead with tough defense and strong
rebounding. Xavier scored eight straight points, part of a 20-4 run
that gave the Musketeers a 25-17 lead with 2:50 to go in the first
half. Dayton cut it to 30-24 at halftime.

	   Both teams shot poorly in the first half, going a combined
2-for-13 on 3-pointers.

	   Xavier was 9-for-28 from the field. Dayton was 9-for-29.
